description:
A SINGLE SEAMLESS PANORAMIC ILLUSTRATION of one continuous interior space — a craftsman's atelier and storage room inside the same antique-dealer's residence as the bureau cabinet of this game. Same warm Art Déco palette, same museum-catalog ink-and-wash rendering. ONE uninterrupted painting, viewed straight-on at eye-level.

FORBIDDEN INSIDE THE IMAGE: no text, no numbers, no labels, no captions, no rulers, no signatures, no watermarks. NO vertical lines, panel borders, triptych dividers, gutters, guidelines or framing strips anywhere — the painting flows continuously from left edge to right edge with no visual cut.

ROOM ARCHITECTURE — a shallow rectangular room with three visible walls:
  • LEFT SIDE WALL angles forward toward the viewer at the very left edge of the image, occupying about the first 8–10% of the image width, visibly receding in 3/4 perspective. Cream plaster, exposed vertical wooden timber posts.
  • BACK WALL faces the viewer head-on, runs across the central majority of the image. Warm aged cream plaster with occasional exposed vertical timber framing posts and diagonal corner braces.
  • RIGHT SIDE WALL mirrors the left, angling forward at the very right edge of the image (last 8–10% of width), receding in 3/4 perspective.
  • CEILING: high (~4m), one continuous exposed dark-wood beam running across the full top of the image, with diagonal corner braces at the back corners.
  • FLOOR: continuous warm wood plank parquet, slightly worn, running unbroken across the entire bottom of the image.

LAYOUT (the image divides MENTALLY — never visibly — into three equal vertical thirds: LEFT, CENTER, RIGHT). The layout fills the room as follows:

— LEFT THIRD (storage area + door anchor):
  • Set INTO the LEFT SIDE WALL, viewed at its 3/4 perspective angle, a beautiful vintage FRENCH ATELIER DOOR (porte d'atelier) painted in PATINATED FOREST-GREEN wood — visibly worn and chipped paint revealing warm raw wood underneath around the brass handle and lower edge. Tall narrow door (~2m tall), vertical wooden planks, simple brass handle at hip height, a small upper window pane (4 small clear glass squares in a 2×2 grid with thin wooden mullions) letting a sliver of soft outdoor light through. The door is partly cropped at the very left edge of the image — only ~70% of its width is visible.
  • AGAINST THE BACK WALL inside the left third, well clear of both the left side wall and the invisible left/center boundary, a TALL INDUSTRIAL METAL STORAGE RACK painted DARK PATINATED FOREST GREEN (saturated dusty green, NOT silver, NOT bare metal): five riveted angle-iron uprights and five horizontal shelves, stacked from near the floor up to about head height. Decoration is sparse: two closed brown cardboard boxes on the second shelf from the top, one small wooden crate on the topmost shelf. The other three shelves are entirely empty. The rack is roughly centered horizontally inside the left third.
  • The floor on the left side is CLEAN (no dust).

— CENTER THIRD (workbench against back wall + centered stained-glass window):
  • PUSHED FLAT AGAINST THE BACK WALL, the LONG LEG of a substantial professional ARTISAN WORKBENCH (établi de menuisier). Real workshop construction: thick worn-oak top (~8–10 cm thick slab), heavy square legs with sturdy lower stretchers, working height (~85–90 cm). The top is bare worn wood, ENTIRELY EMPTY except for a single cast-iron BENCH VICE (étau) bolted to the front edge, roughly centered under the window, with visible turning handle.
  • Above the bench, PERFECTLY HORIZONTALLY CENTERED on the image — its vertical axis of symmetry sits exactly on the 50% midline of the image width — a BEAUTIFUL WIDE WOODEN-FRAMED WINDOW (~1.6 m wide, ~1.5 m tall). Sill resting just above the bench top, panes divided into a clean 3×3 grid (3 vertical columns × 3 horizontal rows) by simple wooden mullions. Crowning the top of this window, a SPECTACULAR ART NOUVEAU STAINED-GLASS IMPOSTE (vitrail), ~30 cm tall, depicting a STYLIZED BLACKSMITH'S ANVIL as the central motif, framed by curving organic Art Nouveau leaves and tendrils. Warm jewel tones — amber, soft forest green, warm cream, hints of brass-gold — with visible black lead came outlining each piece.
  • Below the windowsill, a WIDE WOODEN TABLETTE (~25–30 cm deep) protrudes over the bench top. On it: a TERRACOTTA FLOWERPOT with warm-toned flowers (geraniums, ochre/red petals) on the LEFT side; a TURNED-WOODEN CYLINDRICAL POT holding 4–5 paintbrushes with worn handles on the RIGHT side. The center of the tablette stays uncluttered.
  • Through the clear panes, a small cozy enclosed garden is visible: rosebushes in bloom against a low stone garden wall, climbing roses on a trellis, a paved garden path, terracotta pots.

— RIGHT THIRD (workbench L-wrap + wall shelf + pendant lamp):
  • The SAME workbench continues unbroken. Its LONG LEG, still flat against the back wall, extends from the center third into the right third — same worn-oak top, same legs, same height, same continuous wood grain. Inside the right third, the bench reaches the back-right inner corner of the room and THE TOP WRAPS THE INNER CORNER, smoothly turning 90° to become the SHORT LEG that runs along the RIGHT SIDE WALL in perspective, protruding diagonally toward the foreground-right corner of the image and partly cropping out at the bottom-right corner. This L is ONE single piece of furniture — no joint visible apart from the natural corner wrap. The top of the right portion is bare wood with a few small dark holdfast holes scattered along it (no second vice).
  • AGAINST THE BACK WALL above the right portion of the bench, roughly horizontally centered inside the right third, a SMALL OPEN WOODEN WALL SHELF (~70 cm wide, ~80 cm tall) — two horizontal wooden shelves between two vertical wooden side panels. Decoration: ONE small TERRACOTTA JAR on the top shelf; ONE small plain wooden box on the lower shelf. The shelf is mounted at chest/head height, well above the bench top.
  • HANGING FROM THE CEILING BEAM above the L-corner area of the right third, ONE single INDUSTRIAL VINTAGE METAL WORKSHOP PENDANT LAMP (lustre d'atelier): black/dark enameled-steel conical shade (wide cone, narrower neck), polished brass socket cap, hanging on a dark chain from the ceiling beam at about 1.8 m above the floor. Casts a warm pool of light onto the L-corner.

LAYOUT DISCIPLINE (mental thirds, never drawn): every element of the scene EXCEPT the workbench stays fully INSIDE one single third with clear breathing space on both sides — the green door + storage rack stay inside the LEFT third, the centered window + tablette stays inside the CENTER third, the wall shelf + pendant lamp stay inside the RIGHT third. The ONLY element that crosses an invisible boundary is the workbench, whose long leg runs unbroken along the back wall from inside the center third into the right third (crossing the 66.66% boundary by design). Nothing crosses the 33.33% boundary. NO visible line, mark, gutter, frame, color band, or seam ever indicates these boundaries.

DUST: on the floor of the CENTER and RIGHT portions (the atelier side), faint pale wood dust and a few small scattered wood shavings — subtle and painterly, especially near the workbench legs, around the vice and at the L-corner. Never heavy piles. The LEFT portion floor (storage) stays clean.

DECORATION DISCIPLINE: keep decoration intentionally SPARSE. The only decorative props are: boxes + crate on the storage rack; flowerpot + brush pot on the tablette; jar + small box on the wall shelf. The workbench top and most rack/shelf surfaces stay EMPTY — future game items will be composited onto them.

LIGHTING: soft warm directional light from the upper-left, plus daylight + colored highlights through the centered stained-glass window, plus the warm bulb of the pendant lamp on the right. The atelier side (center + right) is warmer and brighter; the storage side (left) is cleaner and slightly cooler, with the green door catching a soft sliver of light through its small upper panes. No people.

STYLE — identical to the bureau cabinet panorama of this same game: vintage Art Déco illustration, museum-catalog feel, elegant ink line-art with subtle sepia and soft forest-green color wash, cream parchment paper background with subtle paper grain texture, warm wood tones, brass accents. NOT photography, NOT modern realism, NOT 3D render. PNG is fully opaque; no transparent areas, no black bars, no panel borders.
